title: Siddhivinayak Temple, Mumbai
tagline: Siddhivinayak Temple, Mumbai — spiritual town of Maharashtra

overview.short: The Shri Siddhivinayak Ganapati Mandir is a Hindu temple dedicated to Ganesha.
overview.description: The Shri Siddhivinayak Ganapati Mandir is a Hindu temple dedicated to Ganesha. It is located in Prabhadevi neighbourhood of Mumbai, Maharashtra. It was originally built by Laxman Vithu and Deubai Patil on 19 November 1801. It is one of the most popular Hindu temples in Mumbai.

nearestAirport.name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Prabhadevi
topPlaces.description: Prabhadevi (Pronunciation: [pɾəbʱaːd̪eʋiː]) is a small up-scale southern neighbourhood of Mumbai, situated between Dadar to the north, Worli to the south and the Arabian Sea to the west. The area derives its name from the 300-year-old Prabhavati temple located in the locality.

topPlaces.name: Siddhivinayak metro station
topPlaces.description: Siddhivinayak (also known as ICICI Lombard - Siddhivinayak for sponsorship reasons) is an underground metro station located in the Prabhadevi area, on the North–South corridor of the Aqua Line 3 of the Mumbai Metro in Mumbai, India. The station opened to the public on 10 May 2025, under a naming rights agreement between ICICI Lombard and the Mumbai Metro Rail Corporation Ltd (MMRC).

topPlaces.name: Prabhadevi Temple
topPlaces.description: Prabhadevi Temple, also known as Prabhavati Devi Temple, is a Hindu temple located in the Prabhadevi area, near Dadar, Mumbai. The idol representing the main deity, Prabhavati Devi, dates back to the 12th Century.

topPlaces.name: Portuguese Church, Mumbai
topPlaces.description: The Church of Our Lady of Salvation, popularly referred to as Portuguese Church, is one of the oldest churches in Mumbai, India. It is located on S. K. Bole Road in Dadar.

topPlaces.name: Chaitya Bhoomi
topPlaces.description: Chaitya Bhoomi, also written as Chaityabhoomi (IAST: Caityabhūmī, Officially: Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Chaityabhoomi Memorial) is a Buddhist chaitya and the cremation place of B. R. Ambedkar, the architect of the Indian Constitution and the revived Buddhism in India.

topPlaces.name: Three Sixty West
topPlaces.description: Three Sixty West is a skyscraper complex in Mumbai, Maharashtra, India. It comprises two towers, joined at ground level by a podium.

topPlaces.name: The Park (residential project)
topPlaces.description: Lodha Park is an 18.5-acre (7.5 ha) luxury residential skyscraper project which was developed by the Lodha Group in the upscale Lower Parel neighborhood of Mumbai, Maharastra, India. The land for the project was purchased from DLF Limited in 2012 for approximately ₹2,800 crore (₹28,000,000,000).

topPlaces.name: 2017 Mumbai stampede
topPlaces.description: On 29 September 2017, a stampede occurred at the suburban Elphinstone Road railway station in Mumbai, India. At least 23 people were killed and 39 others injured.

items.note: Prabhadevi (Pronunciation: [pɾəbʱaːd̪eʋiː]) is a small up-scale southern neighbourhood of Mumbai, situated between Dadar to the north, Worli to the south and the Arabian Sea to the west. The area derives its name from the 300-year-old Prabhavati temple located in the locality.

Siddhivinayak (also known as ICICI Lombard - Siddhivinayak for sponsorship reasons) is an underground metro station located in the Prabhadevi area, on the North–South corridor of the Aqua Line 3 of the Mumbai Metro in Mumbai, India. The station opened to the public on 10 May 2025, under a naming rights agreement between ICICI Lombard and the Mumbai Metro Rail Corporation Ltd (MMRC).

Prabhadevi Temple, also known as Prabhavati Devi Temple, is a Hindu temple located in the Prabhadevi area, near Dadar, Mumbai. The idol representing the main deity, Prabhavati Devi, dates back to the 12th Century.
The Church of Our Lady of Salvation, popularly referred to as Portuguese Church, is one of the oldest churches in Mumbai, India. It is located on S. K. Bole Road in Dadar.

Chaitya Bhoomi, also written as Chaityabhoomi (IAST: Caityabhūmī, Officially: Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Chaityabhoomi Memorial) is a Buddhist chaitya and the cremation place of B. R. Ambedkar, the architect of the Indian Constitution and the revived Buddhism in India.
Three Sixty West is a skyscraper complex in Mumbai, Maharashtra, India. It comprises two towers, joined at ground level by a podium.

Lodha Park is an 18.5-acre (7.5 ha) luxury residential skyscraper project which was developed by the Lodha Group in the upscale Lower Parel neighborhood of Mumbai, Maharastra, India. The land for the project was purchased from DLF Limited in 2012 for approximately ₹2,800 crore (₹28,000,000,000).
On 29 September 2017, a stampede occurred at the suburban Elphinstone Road railway station in Mumbai, India. At least 23 people were killed and 39 others injured.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Siddhivinayak Temple, Mumbai?
faq.a: The best time to visit Siddhivinayak Temple, Mumbai is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Siddhivinayak Temple, Mumbai?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (9 km) and the nearest railway station is Mumbai CSMT (9 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Siddhivinayak Temple, Mumbai?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Siddhivinayak Temple, Mumbai at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Siddhivinayak Temple, Mumbai?
faq.a: Stays in Siddhivinayak Temple, Mumbai start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Siddhivinayak Temple, Mumbai?
faq.a: The top attractions are Prabhadevi, Siddhivinayak metro station, Prabhadevi Temple, Portuguese Church, Mumbai, Chaitya Bhoomi.
